Featured Work

Two case studies

The clearest picture of how I think: a community treated as a system, and a category launch treated as a campaign.

Case Study 01

An AI-native community engine

Community Growth / Events / Program Manager, SuperOps · Since 2025

A B2B SaaS community treated as a system, where outside signal becomes in-product adoption. Reddit conversations feed an AI-readable content pipeline and a recommendation engine, surfaced through a live Industry Watch space. A Relay.app listener ships daily MSP workflow reviews, every weekday since February.

Tripled daily active users by turning outside signal into in-product adoption.
Case Study 02

Launching an Agentic AI Marketplace

End-to-End Campaign Lead · Jul to Dec 2025

A three-phase launch positioning SuperOps as the category-defining Agentic AI marketplace for MSPs. A mystery Agent Missing teaser, with US billboards, a Nasdaq spot and an Aug 8 drone show, built anticipation. An August community-and-hackathon launch then converted attention into builders, run end to end across paid, social, influencer, content and community.

Built a buzzing builder community: 4,070 visitors, 117 posts, and textbook-marketing praise from the industry.
Campaign gallery
Section · Hackathons

Hackathons

Large-scale, multi-partner program operations with real prize stakes.

SuperHack Global Hackathon

Sep to Dec 2025

A global, AWS-powered hackathon to seed an agentic-AI builder community. Owned the program end to end, from vendor selection and platform setup to judging criteria and a zero-glitch grand-finale livestream. Coordinated AWS, Hack2Skill, leadership and an expert judging panel. Launched in September and closed with a December grand finale.

Program opsSponsor mgmt$100K+ prizes
200+ submissions to 48 finalist teams

SuperHack 2025, Case Study

2025

A public, build-in-the-open case study of how SuperHack 2025 came together. It walks the challenge, the numbers, the team, and the phase-by-phase ownership, then closes on feedback and testimonials. A behind-the-scenes look at running a high-stakes global hackathon.

Section · Moderation & Governance

Moderation & Governance

The systems and standards that keep a community safe, responsive and on-SLA.

Community Moderation Playbook

~2025

Sole-authored, 25-page governance SOP for the SuperOps community on Sprinklr: onboarding prerequisites, a 10-step moderation workflow, a 48-hour escalation process, first and second-level support paths, spam handling and a macro taxonomy.

SOP designSLA & escalationSprinklr
Codifies a repeatable, SLA-backed standard for a B2B SaaS moderation team

Volunteer

Partnered with peer communities and served as a volunteer host for LedByCommunity's weekly webinar series, extending reach across the wider community profession.

VolunteerSlack moderationWebinar host
Section · Community Operations

Community Operations

The machinery, automations, data and integrations, that lets community scale.

  • Performed a community member audit and cleaned up inactive users using Salesforce CRM data, reducing the community from 34K to 7K actual active users.
  • Increased the Time to Respond Rate by directly involving SMEs from the 3rd level to respond in the community, which:
    • Gained trust and credibility
    • Provided direct feedback
    • Reduced the gap between the product team and customers
  • Helped the support team by building an async routing system that:
    • Allowed customers to quickly search existing threads for similar issues
    • Reduced the Product team workload by answering product queries in the public forum
  • Added macros to relevant PoCs so they are notified directly to their inbox, ensuring:
    • No answer is left unanswered within 24 hours
    • Compliance with SLA guidelines
  • Maintained a PoC document covering 30 product lines.
  • Added a rewarding and gamification layer to create ownership among community members.
